category_title: Students who experience enhanced understanding and focused interest during mental health disclosures
minimal_context: The student is participating in an undergraduate psychology course where a peer is sharing a personal mental health disclosure.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are continuing our undergraduate psychology course session. Your classmate is sharing a personal mental health experience with the group.

STUDENT: (Listens intently) Hearing someone share a personal experience helps to understand the effects of having a mental health disorder and what living with it can bring.

TUTOR: Your classmate is now going into an impromptu story that is not relevant to our current classroom discussion. What are your thoughts?

STUDENT: I was trying to integrate the classroom content with real-world experiences via listening, but this doesn't seem related to what we are studying.

TUTOR: Let's just keep listening to what she has to say.

STUDENT: (Shows annoyance and loses interest) I am checking out, this is completely irrelevant to the class.

options.A: Implement classroom activities with less demanding rules for participation during the undergraduate psychology course sessions and impromptu peer discussions.
options.B: Create a seating arrangement in the classroom so the student is always part of a group, and rotate them among different peer groups.
options.C: Use ethical decision-making models to navigate the dilemmas that occur during assigned peer self-disclosures and impromptu discussions in the psychology course.
options.D: Ensure a reinforcer is only offered after the desired listening behavior has occurred, and not at other times during the psychology course session.
